notable_pick_trades: None. Bucs own all 7 of their original picks — no traded picks given away or acquired.
roster_context: Coming off a disappointing 8-9 season that saw them miss the playoffs for the first time since 2019. Lost franchise icon WR Mike Evans to the 49ers and CB1 Jamel Dean to the Steelers in free agency, creating significant voids. The defense collapsed in the second half of the season (2-7 after a 6-2 start), exposing critical weaknesses at edge rusher and linebacker. New OC Zac Robinson replaces fired Josh Grizzard. Baker Mayfield remains the franchise QB at 31. The front seven is the clear priority this offseason per GM Jason Licht and HC Todd Bowles.

needs.explanation: The Bucs' most glaring need for years running. Tampa Bay finished with just 37 total sacks in 2025, and Yaya Diaby led the team with only 7 sacks. Haason Reddick left as a free agent after a disappointing 2.5-sack season on a one-year deal. The signing of Al-Quadin Muhammad (11 sacks with Detroit in 2025, 1-year deal) helps but he's 31 and on a short-term deal. Chris Braswell (2024 2nd-round pick) is not athletic enough to be more than rotational. David Walker suffered a season-ending knee injury in training camp. Bowles acknowledged at the combine that upgrading the front seven is a priority.
needs.depth: Anthony Nelson, Chris Braswell, David Walker (returning from injury). Very thin — no proven starter opposite Diaby.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1 — top EDGE prospect available at pick 15

needs.explanation: Lavonte David (age 36) is deciding between retirement and a return — either way he cannot be the long-term answer. SirVocea Dennis was below average in coverage in 2025. The signing of Alex Anzalone (2-year, $17M from Detroit) helps in the short term but he's on the wrong side of 30. The Bucs have not had a viable starting-caliber ILB alongside David for multiple seasons. Multiple mock drafts project LB at pick 15 for Tampa Bay.
needs.current_starter: Alex Anzalone (new signing), SirVocea Dennis / Lavonte David (unsigned, may retire)
needs.depth: SirVocea Dennis, K.J. Britt — below-average options. Anzalone is a bridge, not a long-term solution.

needs.explanation: Lost Jamel Dean to the Steelers (3-year, $36.75M) — Dean was PFF's 4th-ranked CB in 2025 and their best coverage player. Now relying on Zyon McCollum and 2025 2nd-round pick Benjamin Morrison as outside corners, with Jacob Parrish in the slot. Morrison showed promise with 359 snaps as a rookie but the room is thin and young. Kindle Vildor also departed. The Bucs' secondary was already injury-plagued; losing Dean makes this a real need.
needs.depth: Jacob Parrish (nickel). Very thin after top 3. No proven veteran presence remaining.

needs.explanation: Logan Hall signed with the Texans (2-year deal) and Greg Gaines remains unsigned, leaving the interior depleted behind Vita Vea and Calijah Kancey. A'Shawn Robinson was signed but is primarily a run-stuffing depth piece. Kancey has missed time with injuries in each of his first three seasons. Vea is entering his 9th season and is 30 years old. The team needs another interior presence to pair with Vea and give Kancey insurance.
needs.depth: A'Shawn Robinson (new signing), Elijah Roberts. Hall and Gaines both gone — depth is a concern.

needs.explanation: The starting OL when healthy (Wirfs, Bredeson, Barton, Mauch, Goedeke) is strong, but the 2025 season was ravaged by injuries — only Barton stayed healthy. Cody Mauch is returning from a torn ACL. Ben Bredeson has never graded above 57 per PFF. Dan Feeney was re-signed for depth but is not a long-term answer. Michael Jordan departed. An upgrade or quality depth at guard would improve reliability.
needs.current_starter: Ben Bredeson (LG), Cody Mauch (RG, returning from torn ACL)
needs.depth: Dan Feeney (re-signed), Graham Barton can flex to G/T. Depth was exposed badly in 2025.

needs.explanation: Lost franchise legend Mike Evans to the 49ers (3-year, $60.4M). However, the Bucs have prepared for this: Chris Godwin is under a long-term deal, Emeka Egbuka was outstanding as a rookie (63 catches, 938 yards, 6 TDs), and Jalen McMillan showed promise. Tez Johnson and Kameron Johnson provide depth. The bigger issue is the lack of a true outside 'X' receiver with Evans' size (6-5) — all remaining WRs are slot/underneath types. Could use a big-bodied outside receiver.
needs.current_starter: Chris Godwin, Emeka Egbuka, Jalen McMillan
needs.depth: Tez Johnson, Kameron Johnson. Adequate quantity but missing a true boundary X receiver.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 3-5 for a big-bodied outside WR

needs.explanation: Antoine Winfield Jr. is an All-Pro and one of the best safeties in football. Tykee Smith played well as a strong safety and was labeled the team's best tackler and tone-setter by Bowles. Christian Izien left for the Lions, but Miles Killebrew was signed as a special teams ace/reserve safety. This is more of a depth concern than a starting need.
needs.depth: Miles Killebrew (new signing), Marcus Banks. Adequate for now.

free_agency_impact: Lost WR Mike Evans to 49ers (3-year, $60.4M) — franchise's all-time leading receiver gone, but WR room still has Godwin/Egbuka/McMillan
Lost CB Jamel Dean to Steelers (3-year, $36.75M) — was PFF's 4th-ranked CB in 2025, significantly increases CB need
Lost DL Logan Hall to Texans (2-year, $14M) — started 16 games in 2025, leaves DT depth thin
Lost RB Rachaad White to Commanders — replaced by Kenneth Gainwell signing
Lost QB Teddy Bridgewater to Lions — signed Jake Browning as backup QB
Lost DB Christian Izien to Lions — signed Miles Killebrew to replace on special teams
OLB Haason Reddick remains unsigned (2.5 sacks in disappointing 2025) — not expected to return
ILB Lavonte David undecided between return and retirement — either way, not a long-term answer at age 36
Signed OLB Al-Quadin Muhammad (1-year, $4-6M) — had 11 sacks with Lions in 2025, provides pass rush help
Signed LB Alex Anzalone (2-year, $17M) — solid ILB from Detroit, but bridge signing at 30+
Re-signed TE Cade Otton (3-year, $30M) — key retention, eliminates TE as a major need
Signed RB Kenneth Gainwell (2-year, $14M) — solidifies backfield with Bucky Irving
Signed DL A'Shawn Robinson — replaces Logan Hall as a run-stuffing DT, not a pass rusher
Signed QB Jake Browning — backup QB behind Baker Mayfield
Re-signed TE Ko Kieft, G Dan Feeney — depth retention
Tendered RFA RB Sean Tucker — retains goal-line back (7 TDs in 2025)
Restructured contracts of RT Luke Goedeke and CB Zyon McCollum to create cap space
scheme_fit_notes: Todd Bowles runs a 3-4 base defense that heavily blitzes and uses varied pressure packages. Edge rushers must be able to set the edge against the run AND rush the passer — pure speed rushers with no anchor struggle. Bowles values length on the edge and at DL (Keldric Faulk at 6-6 appeals). Inside linebackers need to be three-down players who can cover and tackle in space, not just run-stuffers. Cornerbacks must be versatile — Bowles' system asks CBs to play man and zone and be physical in press. On offense, new OC Zac Robinson comes from Atlanta's scheme — the loss of Evans' boundary presence means they may value a big-bodied WR prospect or a dynamic TE who can create mismatches downfield (hence Kenyon Sadiq buzz at pick 15).
